## 1. Overview

SiapJasa is a marketplace where customers book on-site services (car wash, cleaning, handyman, errands) from verified providers. Funds are held in escrow and released after provider submits photo proof and a short customer review window elapses.

**Launch scope:** Balikpapan city only. Indonesian language. Rupiah only.

**Goals (phased):**
1. Portfolio/prototype and community learning project for Balikpapan Dev
2. Local tool that neighbors and community members actually use
3. Real business venture with revenue and growth

**Business model:** Discovery marketplace (Model A) at launch. Platform charges a configurable per-category commission from the provider (default 10%). Customers see one all-in price; the fee is not itemized to them.

**Phase 2 intent:** Evolve into a reliability network (Model B) where customers book the platform, not a specific person. Requires minimum provider density per service category (threshold TBD). This structurally eliminates disintermediation.

**Architecture:** Decoupled frontend (React/Vite SPA on Vercel) and backend (Express/TypeScript API on Render.com), communicating via REST. Separate deployments enable independent scaling and team workflows.

**High-level components:**
- **Web client (React PWA on Vercel)** — customer + provider interfaces, installable on Android
- **API server (Express on Render)** — business logic, booking lifecycle, escrow state machine
- **Admin console** — separate React routes (same frontend app, role-gated) for provider approval, disputes, reports, ops
- **PostgreSQL (Render Managed)** — users, providers, services, jobs, bookings, escrow, audit logs
- **Background workers (pg-boss)** — auto-release timers, webhook reconciliation, notification fallback, expiry checks
- **Midtrans** — payment gateway, holds/releases funds (licensed escrow)
- **Cloudflare R2** — photo proof, KTP uploads, credential files
- **Fonnte** — WhatsApp notification fallback
- **BetterStack** — centralized logging, tracing, uptime monitoring
- **PostHog** — product analytics, feature flags

---

## 2. Two Entry Points, One Booking Engine

### 2.1 Browse-and-Book

Customer picks a service category, sees providers sorted by rating and distance, books a slot, pays. Standard marketplace flow.

### 2.2 Post-a-Job (Fixed-Price Open Call)

Customer posts “need X at address Y for Rp Z on date W.” Nearby providers see it; first qualified provider to accept wins. Customer confirms payment after acceptance.

Both paths funnel into the same booking, escrow, photo-proof, and release lifecycle.

---

## 3. Domain Model

### 3.1 User

One account type, distinguished by role flags:
- `Customer` role (everyone has this by default)
- `Provider` role (added after manual admin approval)
- `Admin` role (staff)

A single user can be both customer and provider.

Fields include: `trust_level` enum (`normal` | `warned` | `restricted` | `suspended`). Default is `normal`. Derived from violation history or set manually by admin.

### 3.2 ProviderProfile

One-to-one with User when provider role is granted:
- `display_name`, `bio`, `service_area` (lat/lng + radius in km)
- `kyc_status` (`pending` | `approved` | `rejected`), `kyc_reviewed_by`, `kyc_reviewed_at`
- `ktp_photo_url`, `selfie_photo_url` (private bucket, admin-only access)
- `rating_avg`, `rating_count`, `completed_jobs_count` (aggregated across all skills)
- `is_active` (provider can pause all listings)

### 3.3 ServiceCategory

Editable by admins, not providers. Per-category escrow rules and fees live here:
- `slug` (e.g., `car-wash`, `plumbing-repair`)
- `name_id`, `name_en`
- `review_window_minutes` (e.g., 60 for car wash, 2880 for plumbing)
- `fee_percentage` (e.g., 10.00 for 10%, stored as decimal)
- `requires_photo_proof` (boolean)
- `requires_service_certificate` (boolean)
- `default_price_range` (jsonb: `{min_idr: number, max_idr: number}` — used for post-a-job sanity checks, warns customer if posted price is outside typical range)

### 3.4 ProviderSkill

A provider offers a service under a category. Replaces “Listing” concept:
- `provider_id`, `category_id`
- `title`, `description`, `base_price_idr`, `duration_estimate_minutes`
- `is_active` (can pause individual skills independently)
- Per-skill ratings: `rating_avg`, `rating_count`, `completed_jobs_count`
- Verification: `verification_status` (`unverified` | `pending_review` | `verified` | `revoked`), `verified_by`, `verified_at`, `verification_note`
- `credentials` (jsonb — file URLs for certificates, licenses, etc.)
- `hidden_by_report_id` (nullable — set when reported, cleared on resolution)

A provider can have multiple skills, each with independent ratings and verification status.

### 3.5 Job

The unified booking record for both entry points:
- `customer_id`, `provider_id` (nullable until accepted, for open calls)
- `category_id`, `skill_id` (nullable — null means post-a-job flow)
- `entry_mode` enum: `browse_book` | `open_call`
- `status` enum — see state machine below
- `address`, `lat`, `lng`
- `scheduled_at`, `completed_at`
- `hidden_by_report_id` (nullable)

Price fields live on `Transaction`, not on `Job`.

### 3.6 Job State Machine

```
  (open_call only) → POSTED → ACCEPTED ─┐
  (browse_book)    → BOOKED ────────────┤
                                        ↓
                                   IN_PROGRESS
                                        ↓
                                 PROOF_SUBMITTED
                                   ↓         ↓
                       auto-release    DISPUTED
                        (timer)           ↓
                           ↓          RESOLVED
                       COMPLETED     (refund / release / partial)
```

Terminal states: `COMPLETED`, `CANCELLED`, `EXPIRED` (for unaccepted open calls).

### 3.7 Transaction

Immutable financial snapshot, 1:1 with Job, created at booking/acceptance time:
- `job_id`
- `customer_price_idr` — what the customer agreed to pay (frozen at booking time)
- `currency` (always `IDR` at MVP; explicit for future)
- `platform_fee_pct` — frozen snapshot of `ServiceCategory.fee_percentage` at booking time
- `platform_fee_idr` — computed once: `customer_price_idr * platform_fee_pct`
- `provider_payout_idr` — computed once: `customer_price_idr - platform_fee_idr`
- `listing_price_snapshot_idr` (nullable — for browse_book, the skill’s base_price at booking time)
- `created_at`

Never updated, never deleted. A refund doesn’t modify the Transaction.

### 3.8 EscrowHold

Tracks actual money state, 1:1 with Transaction:
- `transaction_id`
- `midtrans_order_id`, `midtrans_payment_token`
- `status` enum: `pending_payment` | `held` | `released` | `refunded` | `partial_refund` | `failed`
- `amount_held_idr`, `amount_released_idr`, `amount_refunded_idr`
- `held_at`, `released_at`, `refunded_at`
- `last_webhook_at`, `last_webhook_payload` (jsonb)

Mutable — updated as Midtrans webhooks fire. Midtrans is the source of truth.

### 3.9 ProofSubmission

Photo(s) + timestamp + optional note, one-to-many with Job:
- `job_id`, `photo_urls` (jsonb), `note`, `created_at`

### 3.10 EscrowReleaseSchedule

Persisted timer for auto-release:
- `job_id`, `fire_at` (now + category.review_window_minutes), `status` (`pending` | `fired` | `cancelled` | `paused`)

### 3.11 JobEvent (Audit Log — Job State Machine)

Append-only:
- `job_id`, `from_status`, `to_status`
- `actor_id` (nullable — NULL for system actions like auto-release)
- `actor_role` (`customer` | `provider` | `admin` | `system`)
- `reason` (nullable text — required for cancellations, disputes, admin actions, and any transition with dispute potential; min 3 characters)
- `metadata` (jsonb)
- `created_at`

### 3.12 AdminAction (Audit Log — Non-Job Admin Operations)

Append-only:
- `admin_id`
- `action_type` enum (extensible): `provider_approve` | `provider_reject` | `provider_deactivate` | `provider_reactivate` | `skill_verify` | `skill_reject` | `skill_revoke` | `category_create` | `category_update` | `fee_change` | `user_ban` | `user_unban` | `trust_level_override`
- `target_type` (`provider` | `user` | `category` | `provider_skill` | `system`), `target_id`
- `reason` (required text)
- `metadata` (jsonb — e.g., `{"old_is_active": true, "new_is_active": false}`)
- `created_at`

### 3.13 Review

Up to 2 per completed Job (one from customer, one from provider):
- `job_id`, `reviewer_id`, `reviewer_role` (`customer` | `provider`)
- `rating` (1-5), `body` (text — required when rating <= 2, min 3 characters)
- `created_at`
- Unique constraint on `(job_id, reviewer_role)`.

When a customer review is created, denormalized counters update at two levels: the specific `ProviderSkill` and the `ProviderProfile` aggregate. Provider reviews of customers update a `User.customer_rating_avg` / `customer_rating_count` (visible to providers considering an open call).

### 3.14 Report

- `reporter_id`, `reporter_role` (`customer` | `provider`)
- `reported_user_id`, `reported_user_role`
- `context_type` enum: `job_offer` | `posted_job` | `provider_skill` | `completed_job` | `chat_message`
- `context_id` (polymorphic FK)
- `reason_category` enum: `spam` | `scam` | `harassment` | `no_show` | `fraud` | `inappropriate_content` | `other`
- `reason_note` (required, min 3 characters)
- `evidence_urls` (jsonb)
- `status` enum: `open` | `resolved_action_taken` | `resolved_no_action` | `resolved_reporter_at_fault`
- `resolved_by`, `resolved_at`, `resolution_note`
- `created_at`

Unique constraint on `(reporter_id, context_type, context_id)`.

Trust gate: if reporter’s `trust_level` is `normal` or `warned`, the reported entity is auto-hidden. If `restricted`, the report queues for admin review without hiding. Rate limit: max 5 reports per reporter per 24 hours.

### 3.15 UserViolation

Append-only:
- `user_id`
- `source_report_id` (nullable — can come from direct admin observation)
- `violation_type` enum: `false_report` | `spam` | `scam_attempt` | `no_show` | `harassment` | `fraud` | `inappropriate_content` | `other`
- `severity` enum: `minor` | `moderate` | `severe`
- `admin_id`, `admin_note` (required)
- `created_at`

### 3.16 Notification

- `user_id`, `type`, `channel` (`push` | `whatsapp` | `email`)
- `payload`, `status` (`queued` | `sent` | `failed` | `read`)
- `sent_at`, `read_at`

Push sent first; WhatsApp fallback fires after 2 minutes if push fails.

---

## 4. Trust Levels

`User.trust_level` enum: `normal` | `warned` | `restricted` | `suspended`.

Auto-derived from `UserViolation` history:
- 1 moderate OR 2 minor violations → `warned`
- 1 severe OR 2 moderate → `restricted`
- 2 severe OR repeat offenses after warning → `suspended`

Admin can manually override with a required reason (logged as `AdminAction`).

**Effects:**
- `normal` — full access; reports auto-hide reported entity
- `warned` — full access; reports auto-hide; in-app warning shown once
- `restricted` — reports queue without auto-hiding; max 1 open call at a time; skill creation rate-limited
- `suspended` — cannot log in, post, offer, or book; requires admin action to reinstate

Trust level is internal only — not shown publicly to other users.

---

### 5.1 Provider Onboarding

## 5. User Flows

1. User signs up with phone + OTP (or email + password). Account created with `Customer` role.
2. Taps “Become a Provider” → multi-step form: display name, bio, service area (pin on map + radius), KTP photo + selfie, at least one initial skill.
3. Submission → `kyc_status = pending`. User sees “Under review, usually within 24 hours.”
4. Admin reviews, approves or rejects with reason.
5. On approval: provider role flipped on, push + WhatsApp notification sent.

### 5.2 Browse-and-Book (Happy Path)

1. Customer selects category → sees providers sorted by rating x distance.
2. Taps provider → sees skill detail, reviews, photos, verification badge.
3. Taps “Book” → picks date/time, enters address.
4. Confirmation screen with price (fee not itemized to customer).
5. “Pay” → `Job` created (`BOOKED`), `Transaction` frozen, `EscrowHold` initialized, Midtrans payment flow.
6. Midtrans webhook → `EscrowHold.status = held`.
7. Provider notified (push + WhatsApp): “New booking from {customer}.”
8. Provider taps “Start Job” → `IN_PROGRESS`.
9. Provider completes, taps “Submit Proof” → uploads 1-5 photos + optional note → `PROOF_SUBMITTED`.
10. Customer notified: “Review within {review_window_minutes}.”
11. Customer taps “Confirm Complete” → `COMPLETED`, funds released.
OR timer expires → `COMPLETED`, auto-release.
OR customer taps “Dispute” → dispute path.
12. On `COMPLETED`: customer prompted to leave review.

### 5.3 Post-a-Job / Open Call (Happy Path)

1. Customer taps “Post a Job” → picks category, fills title, description, address, date/time, fixed price, optional “verified only” toggle.
2. `Job` created in `POSTED` state (no Transaction yet).
3. Providers with matching skills in range receive push notification.
4. Provider taps “Accept”:
    - Has matching skill → proceed.
    - No matching skill (and job doesn’t require verified) → inline skill creation form (title, description, duration, optional base_price, optional credentials). Max 3 new skills per 24h. Skill created as `unverified`.
    - Job requires verified and provider isn’t → Accept button disabled.
5. Job → `ACCEPTED`, `Transaction` created at posted price, customer gets “pay now” prompt.
6. Customer has 15 minutes to pay. If no payment → acceptance expires, job returns to `POSTED`.
7. On payment → same flow as 5.2 from step 8 onward.
8. Open calls expire after 24 hours (or scheduled time) if unaccepted → `EXPIRED`.

First-to-accept enforced atomically at DB layer.

### 5.4 Dispute Path

1. Customer during review window taps “Dispute” → form: reason category + required note (min 3 chars) + optional photo evidence.
2. Job → `DISPUTED`. Auto-release timer paused. Funds stay held.
3. Notifications: admin (dashboard + push), provider (push + WhatsApp), customer (confirmation).
4. Provider has 24h to respond with counter-evidence.
5. Admin resolves: release in full, refund in full, or partial refund. JobEvent logged with `actor_role = 'admin'`.
6. One party may receive a `UserViolation`.
7. Both parties notified of outcome.

### 5.5 Report & Hide Path

1. User taps “Report” → form: reason category + note (min 3 chars) + optional screenshots.
2. Trust gate: `normal`/`warned` reporter → reported entity auto-hidden. `restricted` reporter → queued without hiding.
3. Rate limit: max 5 reports per reporter per 24h.
4. Admin notified. Reported user notified (can see report category/note but not reporter identity).
5. Admin resolves:
    - `resolved_action_taken` — entity stays hidden, possible violation on reported user.
    - `resolved_no_action` — entity unhidden.
    - `resolved_reporter_at_fault` — entity unhidden, `false_report` violation on reporter.
6. Both parties notified.

### 5.6 Cancellation Flows

All cancellations require a `reason_note` (min 3 characters).

| Scenario | Behavior |
| --- | --- |
| Customer cancels before `IN_PROGRESS` | Full refund, Job → `CANCELLED`. No violation. |
| Customer cancels during `IN_PROGRESS` | Goes to dispute path (provider may have done partial work). |
| Provider cancels before `IN_PROGRESS` | Open call: Job returns to `POSTED`. Browse-book: Job → `CANCELLED`, full refund. Provider gets `minor` violation. |
| Provider cancels during `IN_PROGRESS` | Goes to dispute path. |

### 5.7 Admin Flows

Admin console screens:
- **Dashboard** — pending approvals, open disputes, open reports, daily metrics
- **Provider approvals queue** — review KTP/selfie, approve/reject with reason
- **Skill verification queue** — review credentials, approve/reject with reason
- **Dispute queue** — sorted by age, drill into job history + evidence
- **Report queue** — sorted by age and trust gate status
- **Users** — search, view profile, violation history, trust level, manual override
- **Categories** — CRUD on ServiceCategory including review windows and fee percentages

Every mutating admin action writes an `AdminAction` record with a required reason.

---

## 6. Error Handling & Edge Cases

### 6.1 Payment & Webhook Failures

- Midtrans webhook is the source of truth. App never assumes payment succeeded from client-side alone.
- **Missed webhooks:** Reconciliation cron every 15 min syncs `pending_payment` records older than 10 min against Midtrans API.
- **Duplicate webhooks:** All handlers are idempotent.
- **Refund failures:** Retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff. After 3 failures, alert admin for manual Midtrans dashboard intervention.

### 6.2 Auto-Release Timer

- Persisted in `EscrowReleaseSchedule` table, not in memory.
- Background worker polls every 60s for `fire_at <= now AND status = pending`.
- Checks Job is still in `PROOF_SUBMITTED` before releasing. If already disputed or completed, marks schedule as `cancelled`.
- On dispute: schedule marked `paused`.
- Worker crash recovery: restarts pick up all overdue records.

### 6.3 Race Conditions

- Two providers accepting same open call: atomic DB transaction, only one succeeds.
- Payment arriving after 15-min expiry: immediate refund, job returns to `POSTED`.
- Report filed during active dispute: independent tracks, admin sees both on same page.

### 6.4 Edge Cases

| Scenario | Behavior |
| --- | --- |
| Provider no-shows | Customer taps “Provider didn’t arrive” (requires note, min 3 chars) → `DISPUTED` with `no_show`. Admin fast-tracks refund. Provider gets `moderate` violation. |
| Customer goes unresponsive | Auto-release timer fires. Provider gets paid. |
| Provider submits bogus proof | Customer disputes, admin reviews, rules for customer. Full refund + `severe` violation on provider. |
| Admin doesn’t resolve within SLA | Escalation nag every 12 hours after 48h. |
| Midtrans outage | Booking/payment flows show “temporarily unavailable.” Existing escrows unaffected. Auto-release pauses until health check passes. |
| Same user reports same entity twice | Rejected. Unique constraint on `(reporter_id, context_type, context_id)`. |
| Low-rating review (<=2 stars) | Body text required (min 3 chars): “What went wrong?” |

---

## 7. Notifications

**Primary:** Web push via PWA service worker.
**Fallback:** WhatsApp via Fonnte API — fires after 2 min if push undelivered.

Critical notifications sent via both channels:
- Provider: new booking, new open call, dispute filed, payout released
- Customer: provider accepted, proof submitted, dispute resolved
- Admin: new provider application, new dispute, new report

---

## 8. Background Jobs

| Job | Frequency | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `escrow-release-worker` | Every 60s | Process auto-release timers |
| `midtrans-reconciler` | Every 15 min | Sync missed webhooks |
| `payment-expiry-checker` | Every 5 min | Expire unpaid acceptances (15-min window) |
| `open-call-expiry` | Every 15 min | Expire unaccepted open calls past deadline |
| `notification-fallback` | Every 2 min | Fire WhatsApp fallback for undelivered push |
| `admin-escalation` | Every 12 hours | Nag admin about overdue disputes/reports |

All workers are idempotent, crash-safe (DB-persisted state), and log actions to audit tables.

---

## 9. Testing Strategy

### Tier 1 — Automated, blocks deployment:

- Job state machine transitions (every valid/invalid transition)
- Escrow lifecycle (hold, release, refund, partial refund, idempotent webhooks, auto-release timer)
- Race conditions (concurrent acceptance, late payment)
- Transaction immutability
- Trust gate on reports
- Fee calculation correctness

### Tier 2 — Automated, important:

- Provider onboarding flow
- Inline skill creation during open-call acceptance
- Notification fan-out
- Report → hide → resolve → unhide lifecycle
- Review window enforcement
- Cancellation rules

### Tier 3 — Manual at MVP:

- Midtrans sandbox end-to-end
- WhatsApp fallback delivery
- PWA install + push on Android
- Admin console UX
- Map/location accuracy

### Test types:

- **Unit tests** — fee calculation, state machine validator, review window checks, trust level derivation
- **Integration tests** — full job lifecycle, dispute lifecycle, report lifecycle, concurrent acceptance, audit trail correctness (real DB, mocked external APIs)
- **E2E / Sandbox tests** — Midtrans sandbox payment flow, webhook failure + reconciler catch-up

---

## 10. Tech Stack

**Architecture:** Decoupled frontend and backend — separate repos/deployments, communicating via REST API. This enables independent scaling, independent deployments, and different team members working on each layer without conflicts.

### 10.1 Frontend

| Layer | Choice | Why |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Framework | React 19 + TypeScript | User-specified. Strong ecosystem, Balikpapan Dev volunteers most likely to know React. |
| Build | Vite | User-specified. Fast dev server, fast builds, modern ESM-first tooling. |
| PWA | vite-plugin-pwa (Workbox) | Adds service worker, install prompt, offline shell, push notifications to the Vite build. |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui | Fast, consistent, low learning curve. No custom design system at MVP. |
| HTTP Client | ky (or axios) | Lightweight, supports interceptors for auth token injection and error handling. |
| State Management | TanStack Query (React Query) | Server-state caching, optimistic updates, auto-retry. Avoids manual loading/error state boilerplate. |
| Forms | React Hook Form + Zod | Type-safe validation shared with backend Zod schemas (via shared package or copy). |
| Maps | Google Maps API (@vis.gl/react-google-maps) | Service area pin + radius, job location display. Indonesian standard. |
| Routing | React Router v7 | Standard for Vite + React SPAs. File-based routing optional via plugin. |
| Deployment | Vercel | User-specified. Zero-config for Vite SPAs, global CDN, preview deployments per PR. |

### 10.2 Backend

| Layer | Choice | Why |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Runtime | Node.js + TypeScript | User-specified. Shared language with frontend. |
| Framework | Express.js | Battle-tested, largest middleware ecosystem, natural pair with Sequelize. |
| ORM | Sequelize v6 + sequelize-typescript | User-specified. Mature, decorator-based models, migration CLI built in. |
| Validation | Zod | Request validation middleware. Schemas can be shared with frontend for type alignment. |
| Auth | Better Auth | Phone OTP, social login, session management. Has Express adapter. Self-hosted, no vendor lock-in. |
| API Style | REST (JSON) | Simple, well-understood, easy to debug. OpenAPI spec generated via swagger-jsdoc for frontend contract. |
| Background Jobs | pg-boss | Postgres-backed job queue. Scheduling (auto-release timers), retries, cron-like recurring jobs. No Redis dependency. |
| Containerization | Docker | User-specified. Dockerfile for consistent dev/staging/prod environments. docker-compose for local dev (app + Postgres + pg-boss). |
| Deployment | Render.com | User-specified. Docker-native deployment, managed Postgres available, auto-deploy from git, free tier for MVP. |

### 10.3 Database

| Layer | Choice | Why |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Engine | PostgreSQL 16 | Relational core, jsonb for metadata/credentials, PostGIS extension for geo queries (service area matching). |
| Hosting | Render Managed PostgreSQL | Co-located with backend on Render for low latency. Free tier (1 GB) covers MVP. Upgrade to dedicated when scaling. |
| Migrations | Sequelize CLI (sequelize-cli) | Model-driven migrations, up/down support, seed scripts for dev data. |

### 10.4 External Services

| Service | Choice | Why |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Payments | Midtrans | Indonesian standard. GoPay, OVO, DANA, bank transfer, credit card. Has hold/release (escrow) API. Sandbox mode for dev. |
| Object Storage | Cloudflare R2 | Photo proof, KTP uploads, credentials. S3-compatible API. No egress fees. |
| WhatsApp | Fonnte API | Indonesian WhatsApp gateway. Cheap, reliable, simple REST. No Meta Business API overhead at MVP. |
| Maps | Google Maps API | Geocoding, distance matrix, static maps for notifications. |
| Email (transactional) | Resend | For email receipts, password resets, admin alerts. Free tier covers MVP volume. |

### 10.5 Ops & Observability

| Layer | Choice | Why |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Tracing & Logging | BetterStack | User-specified. Centralized log aggregation + uptime monitoring. Structured JSON logs from Express piped to BetterStack via their Node.js SDK. |
| Product Analytics | PostHog | User-specified. Event tracking (bookings, conversions, funnel analysis), session recordings for UX debugging, feature flags for gradual rollouts. Self-hostable if needed later. |
| Feature Flags | PostHog (built-in) | User-specified. Gradual feature rollout (e.g., enable bidding for 10% of users), kill switches, A/B testing. |
| Error Tracking | BetterStack (or Sentry free tier) | Unhandled exception capture in both frontend and backend. BetterStack covers this via log-based alerting; add Sentry if richer stack traces needed. |
| Uptime | BetterStack (built-in) | Heartbeat monitoring for API health endpoint + Midtrans webhook endpoint. Alerts via Slack/WhatsApp/email. |
| CI/CD | GitHub Actions | Lint + typecheck + test on PR. Auto-deploy to Render (backend) and Vercel (frontend) on merge to main. |

### 10.6 Local Development

```
docker-compose.yml:
  - postgres:16 (with PostGIS)
  - app (backend, hot-reload via ts-node-dev or tsx)

Frontend:
  - vite dev server (port 5173), proxying API calls to localhost:3000
```

Environment parity: Docker ensures local matches Render. Frontend talks to local backend via Vite’s proxy config — no CORS issues in dev.

---

## 11. Phase Boundaries

### MVP (Phase 1) — “Discovery marketplace with escrow”

- Decoupled architecture: React/Vite PWA (Vercel) + Express/TypeScript API (Render.com)
- Customer and provider registration (manual KYC)
- Provider skills with per-skill ratings and verification
- Browse-and-book flow
- Post-a-job (fixed-price open call) with inline skill creation
- Escrow via Midtrans (hold → photo proof → auto-release timer)
- Dispute resolution (admin-mediated)
- Mutual reporting with trust levels
- Admin console with full audit logging
- PWA + push + WhatsApp fallback (Fonnte)
- BetterStack logging/tracing + PostHog analytics/feature flags
- Balikpapan only, IDR only, Indonesian language

### Phase 1.5 — “Bidding & chat”

- Bidding model for open calls (when user demand surfaces)
- In-app messaging (if WhatsApp deep-links prove insufficient)
- iOS PWA push improvements

### Phase 2 — “Reliability network”

- Customers book the platform, not a specific person
- Automatic provider assignment (availability + rating + proximity + verification)
- Provider scheduling / availability calendar
- Automated KYC (Xendit or Privy)
- Quality standards per category
- SLA enforcement
- Richer trust scoring (completion rate, response time, dispute ratio)

### Phase 3 — “Scale”

- Multi-city expansion
- React Native mobile apps
- Subscription/package model for recurring services
- Provider tiers
- Analytics dashboard for providers
- Event services vertical (second vertical)
- Third-party API

---

## 12. Design Rationale

### 12.1 Disintermediation Analysis

| Vertical | Implementation | Competitors (Balikpapan) | Profit | Demand | Disintermediation Risk |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Home services (recurring) | Medium | Gojek weak locally | Low margin | High | HIGH — WhatsApp after job 1 |
| Home repairs (one-off) | Medium | Gojek GoFix weak locally | Good | High | LOW — hopes it never breaks again |
| Delivery | Hard | Gojek/Grab dominate | Thin | High | N/A — don’t compete |
| Tutors/lessons | Easy | Ruangguru online | Medium | High | VERY HIGH — WhatsApp after lesson 1 |
| Event services | Medium | Instagram chaos | Excellent | Medium-high | LOW — one-off by nature |

**Decision:** Hyperlocal services chosen as launch vertical despite disintermediation risk. Mitigated by Model A (discovery-first, accept disintermediation at MVP) with designed transition to Model B (reliability network) at Phase 2, structurally eliminating disintermediation by making the platform the booking target rather than individual providers.

### 12.2 Escrow Rules Differ by Service Category

A generic one-size-fits-all escrow rule fails both providers and customers:
- Car wash (60 min window): customer is present, verification is fast. Making a car washer wait 48h for payment is hostile.
- Home cleaning (240 min window): customer might discover missed spots after the cleaner leaves.
- Plumbing/electrical (2880 min / 48h window): hidden defects appear later. A 60-minute window under-protects customers.
- Errands/delivery (120 min window): moderate verification time.

Solution: `ServiceCategory.review_window_minutes` is configurable per category. New categories inherit a sensible default (240 min) and admins tune it. No schema changes needed when adding categories.

### 12.3 Why Manual Provider Onboarding at MVP

- Sends a strong trust signal to early customers
- Ali personally vetting the first cohort means learning the supply side intimately
- Informs the Phase 2 reliability-network quality standards
- At MVP volume (<20 signups/week), manual review is sustainable
- Revisit when volume exceeds ~20 signups/week: introduce automated KYC as a pre-filter, keep manual review as a secondary check
